api_contract.keep_in_python: task definitions, experiment orchestration, evidence exports, baselines, paper tables, PyNN populations/connectors, standard spike readback

api_contract.customize_in_c: persistent state, delayed-credit queues, eligibility/dopamine/plasticity, compact summaries, lifecycle state, routing/memory kernels when promoted
api_contract.role: only for substrate mechanics PyNN cannot express or scale as closed-loop on-chip state

api_contract.keep_in_python: offline analysis, registry, preflight, JobManager packaging, result ingestion
api_contract.role: commands, configuration, validation, logging, and comparison to canonical software baselines

api_contract.required_before_hardware_learning: event-indexed spike delivery, lazy/active trace decay, compact state readback, build/load/command acceptance
api_contract.role: hardware proof only after scale blockers are resolved

criteria.name: direct custom-runtime hardware learning is blocked
PyNN/sPyNNaker boundary remains explicit
criteria.value: PyNN/sPyNNaker primary, C only for unsupported substrate mechanics

memory_budget.scale_note: current linked-list allocation; production should use bounded pool/index
current post-list allocation; production needs pre-index and active-trace metadata
static bounded state; acceptable at current scaffold size
compact summary state; should be expanded intentionally for readback

runtime_complexity.required_fix: add pre-indexed outgoing adjacency: pre_id -> compact outgoing synapse/event list
use lazy timestamp decay or an active-trace list updated only for recently touched synapses
modulate only active trace list or lazily evaluated eligible synapses
add direct neuron id -> neuron/state index table or bounded preallocated pool

runtime_complexity.current_complexity: allocation-time variable cost and fragmentation risk
runtime_complexity.required_fix: preallocate bounded pools with free lists/active masks and explicit capacity telemetry

runtime_complexity.current_complexity: insufficient observability, not a runtime cost issue
runtime_complexity.required_fix: add fragmented or compact state-summary readback for spikes, reward, pending, slots, and weights
define core-local shard contract, per-core summaries, and inter-core routing/resource budgets
keep bounded; increase only with explicit memory-budget artifact
use due-time ring/event queue if P grows beyond the fixed budget
shard across cores and keep N per core within measured budget

scale_blockers.current_pattern: incoming pre spike scans every post-entry and every synapse
scale_blockers.required_fix: add pre-indexed outgoing adjacency: pre_id -> compact outgoing synapse/event list
scale_blockers.why_it_matters: large CRA populations would spend most runtime scanning irrelevant synapses

scale_blockers.current_pattern: timer tick sweeps every synapse to decay eligibility traces
scale_blockers.required_fix: use lazy timestamp decay or an active-trace list updated only for recently touched synapses
scale_blockers.why_it_matters: all-synapse trace decay destroys the expected SpiNNaker speed advantage as S grows

scale_blockers.required_fix: modulate only active trace list or lazily evaluated eligible synapses
scale_blockers.why_it_matters: global reward updates should touch active traces, not every synapse

scale_blockers.current_pattern: each delivered synaptic input performs a linked-list neuron lookup
scale_blockers.required_fix: add direct neuron id -> neuron/state index table or bounded preallocated pool
scale_blockers.why_it_matters: even after adding outgoing adjacency, input delivery can stay expensive

scale_blockers.complexity: allocation-time variable cost and fragmentation risk
scale_blockers.current_pattern: runtime birth/death and synapse creation use sark_alloc/sark_free linked structures
scale_blockers.required_fix: preallocate bounded pools with free lists/active masks and explicit capacity telemetry
scale_blockers.why_it_matters: long-running organism growth should not depend on heap fragmentation behavior

scale_blockers.complexity: insufficient observability, not a runtime cost issue
scale_blockers.current_pattern: READ_SPIKES returns only neuron count and low 16 bits of timestep
scale_blockers.required_fix: add fragmented or compact state-summary readback for spikes, reward, pending, slots, and weights
scale_blockers.why_it_matters: learning acceptance needs compact summaries/readback, not just count/timestep

scale_blockers.current_pattern: single-core global state and no shard/placement contract
scale_blockers.required_fix: define core-local shard contract, per-core summaries, and inter-core routing/resource budgets
scale_blockers.why_it_matters: population growth requires shardable per-core state and host-visible partitioning

static_checks.name: PyNN/sPyNNaker boundary documented
static_checks.rule: runtime README keeps C as experimental sidecar

static_checks.name: custom C is limited to unsupported/scaling substrate pieces
static_checks.rule: docs must not imply full repo rewrite in C

static_checks.name: bounded context slots
static_checks.rule: defined and used as fixed array

static_checks.name: bounded pending horizons
static_checks.rule: defined and used as fixed array

static_checks.name: pending horizons do not store future target
count-only readback is explicitly marked incomplete

summary.claim_boundary: Scale-readiness audit only; PASS means blockers are explicit, not that the runtime is scale-ready.
summary.custom_c_role: only CRA-specific on-chip state, delayed credit, plasticity, lifecycle/routing, and compact readback that PyNN cannot express or scale
summary.next_step_if_passed: Tier 4.22g event-indexed spike delivery plus lazy/active eligibility traces before any custom-runtime learning hardware claim.
summary.pynn_spynnaker_role: primary hardware construction/mapping/run layer for supported primitives
